recapPrompt.prompt: When two intervals overlap, how do we find the shared part?

choices.label: Use the bigger start and the smaller end
choices.feedback: Correct. The shared part begins later and ends earlier.

choices.label: Use the smaller start and the bigger end
choices.feedback: Not this one. That would give the outer merged range, not the shared part.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Explain in your own words why sorting by start time helps so many interval problems.

practiceChallenge.title: Mastery check: rebuild the interval clues
practiceChallenge.description: Complete the helper so it returns the main interval clue for each kind of job.
hints: Overlap depends on how starts compare to ends.
Merge problems often sort by start time first.
Meeting-room problems track the earliest ending active meeting.
practiceChallenge.successMessage: Pass. You rebuilt the main interval clues from memory.
practiceChallenge.retryMessage: Needs practice. Recheck the overlap rule, the merge-first step, and the meeting-room idea.
